title: Lovely Charred Spanish Lamb Tagine
description: A romantic Spanish dish featuring charred lamb, cumin, and a rich tagine sauce, made in a traditional clay tagine vessel and spherified for a unique twist, with anti-inflammatory friendly ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Heat the olive oil in the clay tagine vessel over medium high heat.
Sear the lamb pieces until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes, then remove from the vessel and set aside.
Add the cumin, smoked paprika, and garlic to the vessel and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lemon juice and chicken broth to the vessel, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.
Return the lamb to the vessel, cover with a lid, and transfer to the preheated oven.
Braise the lamb for 2 1/2 hours, or until tender and falling apart.
While the lamb is braising, prepare the spherification bath by mixing the sodium alginate and water in a bowl.
Once the lamb is done, remove it from the vessel and shred it into bite sized pieces.
Using a syringe, drop small amounts of the lamb mixture into the spherification bath, creating small pearls of lamb.
Serve the charred lamb tagine hot, garnished with the lamb pearls and a sprinkle of cumin.
